strengths: The section still reads as one idea with ArchSingle carrying the lead and restrained support around it.
The active footprint is broad enough to register at scene scale.
Density is consistent across the sampled window (moderate).
Multiple families are present, but the support family does not overpower the lead.
The section keeps a readable lead target even while support is present.
weaknesses: Section development is too flat across opening, middle, and closing slices.
designAdjustmentSuggestions: Clarify how the section should evolve from opening to closing instead of holding one static treatment.

strengths: Model balance is weighted enough to preserve a clear lead-plus-support hierarchy.
Scene spread is large enough to support broader composition.
Frame-to-frame density remains stable through the sampled checkpoint.
Family spread is broad enough without losing the lead-target hierarchy.
Centroid motion indicates the active content is evolving, not frozen.
Lead-target hierarchy remains intact across the section.
weaknesses: Section contrast is underdeveloped; the pass is not evolving enough over time.
revisionSuggestions: Introduce a bounded section-level evolution in spread, density, or support role across the sampled window.

nextMoves.instruction: Clarify how the section should evolve from opening to closing instead of holding one static treatment.
Introduce a bounded section-level evolution in spread, density, or support role across the sampled window.
